title: EngiFoundry Executor and Reviewer Configuration
description: Explains every Executor, Reviewer, and Gate field. Agents must read this complete contract together with executors.json before arranging execution or Review.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the project-owned Executor and Reviewer configuration.

schemaRef.description: Project-relative path to this complete field contract.

configured.description: True only after the complete four-question configuration flow commits successfully.

executor.description: The single configured implementation Executor. There is no automatic Executor order or silent fallback list.

reviewer.description: The configured Reviewer mechanism. It is still eligible only when it starts in a genuinely fresh context, can inspect the complete subject and evidence, and did not produce the reviewed work.

gate.description: Mandatory execution gates. Every declared Gate is binding policy, not advice. An Agent must evaluate and obey every Gate before proceeding.

executorUnavailable.description: Facts that govern objective Executor unavailability. A missing command, failed authentication, unavailable pinned model, or inability to start the verified invocation activates this Gate. A completed invocation with failing tests or incorrect output is an execution failure, not unavailability.

action.description: Stop the task and ask the user before any fallback. Never silently switch Executor.

fallbackTarget.description: The existing controlling Agent session may take over only after user approval.

decisionScope.description: Approval applies only to the current task and does not rewrite project configuration.

executorId.description: Canonical stable identifier resolved for the CLI selection.

kind.description: The configured selection is invoked through a CLI.

label.description: Canonical human-readable name confirmed during configuration.

command.description: Verified executable name. User free text is never executed directly.

modelMode.description: Whether the CLI chooses its default model or receives an explicitly verified canonical model ID.

model.description: Canonical verified model ID. Required when modelMode is pinned.

originalDescription.description: Unchanged user description retained for a successfully resolved custom selection and displayed as a later Reviewer option when applicable.

usage.description: Verified non-interactive invocation template with a {prompt} placeholder and, when the CLI supports an explicit working-directory argument, a {workspace} placeholder. Invocation otherwise starts with the project root as its process working directory.
